sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Routine follow-up of chronic problems.
History of Present Illness
Joseph is a 59-year-old male here for routine follow-up of his chronic problems. He reports managing through depression and his asthma has been acting up due to a really bad pollen season. He is keeping his diabetes under control, but he's been experiencing persistent issues all around. He reports traveling all over to different sports tournaments for the kids, so he's been eating on the go and has been eating on the go, with a weekend of eating on the go, crumby junk food, pizza, and didn't really stick to the diet, so that was a bit of an adjustment. He reports feeling lightheaded when running up and down the stairs, and he did get a little dizzy, but he thought it was just the heat and the fatigue. He reports knee pain from running and picking up boxes. He's been weening himself off of prozac, and he's been using his inhaler only when it seems to go over about 85 degrees out. He's been traveling to different sports tournaments for the kids. He reports that he's been eating on the go.
Findings

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Physical exam reveals no apparent distress. The patient's neck is normal. Thyroid is not enlarged. Lungs exhibit bilateral expiratory wheezing, which is very faint. Heart rhythm is regular. Abdomen is soft. Lower extremities have no edema. Chest x-ray is normal. Hemoglobin a1c is over the past couple months. Dragon shows pfts are good.
Assessment
The patient has chronic depression, asthma, and diabetes mellitus.
Plan
Increase metformin to 1000 mg twice a day. Follow up in three months to repeat hemoglobin a1c. Continue albuterol two puffs every four to six hours as needed. Add symbicort two puffs twice a day during the summer. Start zoloft at 25 mg once a day. The nurse will get you situated with all of that. Schedule follow-up in four weeks if symptoms persist.
